rationale: The Iran deal progress is a mild positive energy signal for Poland, while confirmed US NATO asset cuts remain the key security concern; no qualitative change in either front this cycle.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Confirmed US NATO asset reductions directly weaken the eastern flank deterrence Poland relies on. No new escalation this cycle, but the baseline security concern persists.

top_events.title: Iran says deal to end fighting with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal would ease global oil and gas prices, offering indirect relief on energy costs for Polish households. Still no signing and no timeline confirmed.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: Putin's rare admission of domestic economic strain from the war is a mild signal that Russian war capacity may be under pressure, marginally positive for Poland's security outlook.
